mnemonics: SIG-D: Signals Interrupt, Daemons Detach (Remember that signals are asynchronous interrupts, while daemons are processes that detach from the controlling terminal)
keyPoints: Signals are software interrupts delivered to a process
Daemon processes run in the background, detached from any terminal
The kill() system call sends signals to processes
commonMistakes: Not handling SIGCHLD leading to zombie processes
Forgetting to detach from the controlling terminal when creating daemons
Using signal() instead of sigaction() for reliable signal handling
